scope: Artifact-only triage of global test baseline failure observed after failed controlled remediation apply attempt.
global_test_failure: fatal: path 'CURRENT_STATE.md' exists on disk, but not in 'HEAD'
triage_reasoning: The observed Project_ARIS HEAD still matches the failed-apply artifact (6cec74deb7a99b7eb227df84a902650ca092e00f), so the triage was performed against the same documented baseline.
CURRENT_STATE.md is present on disk and tracked in the current Project_ARIS HEAD, which means the recorded fatal path error is not explained by any residual S3 remediation patch after rollback.

Focused no-patch tests already fail for unrelated baseline reasons: test_if08_w05_preflight_readiness_rerun expects active_next_phase=IF-08, test_if08_w05_controlled_execution expects a W0.5 canonroadmap definition, and test_if08_w05_post_sync_review is blocked instead of pass.

Full unittest discovery re-emits the CURRENT_STATE.md fatal message in the middle of many unrelated errors and failures, which indicates a pre-existing unstable global baseline rather than a bounded regression introduced by the reverted local remediation apply.

The nested aris-active-context worktree has an uncommitted README.md diff that was intentionally excluded from staging, and its validator already fails with 'project post-sync decision must be pass', so canonical PASS conditions are not satisfied.
blocked_reason: Project_ARIS global baseline is already failing outside the authorized remediation surface, and aris-active-context validator currently fails before canonical PASS conditions can be met.
